claimText: The World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.
evidenceExcerpt: Established in 1950 as a specialized agency of the United Nations, headquartered in Geneva.
examUse: Statement-based question on UN specialized agencies and their headquarters.
distractorHooks: UN principal organ instead of specialized agency
Headquartered in New York or Paris

claimText: India is a member state of the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO).
evidenceExcerpt: WMO is a specialized UN agency established in 1950, located in Geneva, Switzerland, with 193 Member States including India.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's membership in international organizations.

claimText: The Global Atmosphere Watch (GAW) Programme is an initiative coordinated by the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) for global atmospheric monitoring.
evidenceExcerpt: Coordinates global atmospheric monitoring networks through the Global Atmosphere Watch (GAW) Programme.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching initiatives to their parent organizations.

claimText: The World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) publishes the Greenhouse Gas Bulletin, State of the Global Climate, and State of Global Water Resources reports.
evidenceExcerpt: Publishes the Greenhouse Gas Bulletin, State of the Global Climate, and State of Global Water Resources.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on reports and publishing organizations.

claimText: The Greenhouse Gas Bulletin 2024 reported a 54% increase in radiative forcing from long-lived greenhouse gases.
evidenceExcerpt: Greenhouse Gas Bulletin 2024 reported CO2 levels at 423.9 ppm and a 54% increase in radiative forcing from long-lived greenhouse gases.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent environmental data and reports.
distractorHooks: Short-lived climate pollutants caused the increase
